confidence_framework.T1: Primary government source (SEBI circulars, gazette notifications, official regulations)
confidence_framework.T2: Industry publication (TaxGuru, Economic Times, Business Standard, legal databases)

executive_summary.what_is_egr: Electronic Gold Receipt (EGR) is a digital certificate representing ownership of physical gold stored in a SEBI-registered vault, notified as a 'security' under Section 2(h)(iia) of the Securities Contracts (Regulation) Act, 1956. EGRs are traded on stock exchanges (BSE, NSE) and can be converted back to physical gold.
executive_summary.regulatory_overview: The EGR ecosystem comprises Vault Managers (custody), Depositories (NSDL/CDSL), Stock Exchanges (BSE/NSE), Clearing Corporations, and Trading Members. SEBI is the sole regulator.
executive_summary.status: BSE launched EGR trading in October 2022; NSE launched in May 2026. As of now, three Vault Managers are registered: Brink's India Pvt Ltd (Mumbai), Malca-Amit JK Logistics Pvt Ltd (Mumbai), and Sequels Logistics Pvt Ltd (Ahmedabad).

eligibility.net_worth: Minimum Rs. 50 crore (as per VM Regulations 2021); note: SEBI Custodian Regulations amended in Sept 2025 raised net worth for custodians generally to Rs. 75 crore with 3-year transition for existing entities. Vault Managers should confirm if this amendment applies to them specifically.
eligibility.infrastructure: Adequate office space, vaults for safe deposit/storage/withdrawal of gold, computer systems capability, and technology for real-time tracking
eligibility.sop: Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) for all recognized vaults covering security, storage, withdrawal, creation/extinguishment of EGRs
eligibility.source: T1: SEBI (Vault Managers) Regulations, 2021; T2: TaxGuru summary

application_process.form: Form A of the First Schedule under VM Regulations, 2021
application_process.application_fee: Non-refundable fee of Rs. 1,00,000 (one lakh rupees)
application_process.registration_fee: Rs. 10,00,000 (ten lakh rupees) at the time of grant of certificate
application_process.renewal_fee: Rs. 10,00,000 every three years, payable within three months before expiry of the block period
application_process.source: T1: SEBI VM Regulations 2021, Second Schedule

documents_required: Net Worth certificate (not older than 3 months from date of application)
Copy of audited financial statements for last two financial years
Details of infrastructure (office space, vaults, IT systems)
Details of competent personnel and their experience
Proof of incorporation and corporate structure
Source: T1: VM Regulations 2021; T2: TaxGuru analysis

fsd_requirement.description: Fixed Sum Deposit (FSD) of Rs. 10,00,000 to be furnished to either NSDL or CDSL before obtaining certificate of registration from SEBI. This minimum FSD must be maintained at all points in time.
fsd_requirement.source: T1: SEBI Standard Operating Guidelines for VM & Depositories; T2: TaxGuru

ongoing_compliance.net_worth_maintenance: Must maintain minimum net worth at all times during validity of registration
ongoing_compliance.quarterly: Compliance Officer must issue quarterly declaration to Depository within 7 working days from quarter end, confirming compliance with all applicable norms
ongoing_compliance.reconciliation: Vault Manager and Depositories must regularly reconcile data of EGRs created with physical gold in vaults
ongoing_compliance.physical_verification: Depositories must carry out physical verification of gold stored in recognized vaults at least once in a fortnight; full inspection of Vault Manager and vaults annually
ongoing_compliance.records_retention: All records and documents must be preserved for a minimum period of 5 financial years
ongoing_compliance.inspection: SEBI may appoint inspecting officers with at least 10 days' notice; may appoint auditors to inspect books and gold deposits
ongoing_compliance.source: T1: SEBI VM Regulations 2021; T1: SOG for VM & Depositories

depository.role: NSDL and CDSL hold EGRs in dematerialized form; create ISINs; provide common interface for VM interaction
depository.source: T1: SEBI Master Circular for EGRs, June 2024

stock_exchange_trading_member.note: Trading Members (stock brokers) must be registered with SEBI and have membership on the stock exchange offering EGR segment. Capital requirements for brokers are governed by SEBI (Stock Brokers) Regulations and exchange-specific net worth norms, not EGR-specific regulations.

vault_manager_registration.timeline: SEBI evaluates application based on completeness; no statutory timeline specified for grant, but typically 3-6 months for intermediaries

fees_and_timelines.source: T1: SEBI VM Regulations 2021, Second Schedule

precedents.source: T2: TaxGuru analysis article (June 2026); T2: Economic Times

gaps.gap: Exact capital requirements for Trading Members (stock brokers) in the EGR segment — whether any EGR-specific deposit or margin requirements exist beyond standard broker norms
gaps.action: Query SEBI circular on 'Risk Management Framework in EGR segment' dated April 11, 2022 and any subsequent amendments

gaps.gap: Whether the Sept 2025 amendment to Custodian Regulations (raising net worth to Rs. 75 crore) has been explicitly applied to Vault Managers under VM Regulations, or if VM Regulations retain the Rs. 50 crore threshold
gaps.action: Review SEBI VM Regulations amendment history post-Feb 2025

gaps.gap: Current processing time for VM registration applications at SEBI — no statutory timeline is specified in the regulations
gaps.action: Check SEBI internal processing norms or recent application precedents

gaps.gap: Detailed IT audit requirements for Vault Managers — the regulations mention computer systems capability but do not specify exact cybersecurity/audit standards
gaps.action: Review SEBI circulars on IT infrastructure for intermediaries and any VM-specific IT audit guidelines

gaps.gap: Trading volumes and liquidity metrics for EGRs on BSE/NSE to assess market viability
gaps.action: Pull exchange trading data or AMFI/SEBI market reports
